sections.paragraph:
- Original: ὄφελον

- Transliteration: Ophelon

- Phonetic: of'-el-on

- Definition:

1. would that, where one wishes that a thing had happened which has not happened or a thing be done which probably will not be done

- Origin: first person singular of a past tense of G3784

- TDNT entry: None

- Part(s) of speech:

- Strong's: First person singular of a past tense of G3784; I ought (wish) that is (interjectionally) oh that!: - would (to God).
